skill.name: viral-hooks
skill.description: Create and critique short-form video hooks for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts. The skill generates hook batches, evaluates weak openings, and maps each idea to visual, verbal, and on-screen text layers.
skill.summary: Generate and improve short-form video hooks across proven creator frameworks.
skill.author: vyralcontent

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high and medium issues, but review found the external-command, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance hits are Markdown references, templates, or ordinary marketing-language terms, not executable code. The only confirmed concern is low severity: the skill contains hardcoded external links and promotional language for Vyral, which should be disclosed but does not indicate malware or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External Marketing Links
low_findings.description: The skill includes a remote image/link and promotional references to Vyral. This is a low marketplace transparency concern because it can send users to an external site, but I did not find evidence of automatic code execution, credential collection, or hidden data transfer.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external domain and URL are directly visible in the reviewed skill text. The risk is limited because the content is Markdown and no executable network client code is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to Markdown inline-code references and fenced templates. They name other skill files, archetype labels, or worksheet fields; they do not contain Ruby code, shell backtick execution, or command invocation logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed files are Markdown documentation and templates, and the flagged backticks are formatting markers. No executable source file, shell command, eval path, or user-input command construction was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Content Terms Misread as Weak Crypto
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings appear to match ordinary words in hook-writing guidance, such as hook tactics, timing, and framework references. No cryptographic API, hashing function, cipher choice, password storage, or security-sensitive implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are prose about video hooks and content structure. I found no code that performs encryption, hashing, signing, password handling, or protocol configuration.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Content Workflow Language Misread as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings are normal creator-workflow language, including timing checks, hook testing, and audience targeting guidance. I did not find host inspection, environment discovery, network scanning, or filesystem reconnaissance behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each cited location is Markdown guidance for video-hook creation or critique. There is no script, command, API call, or instruction to inspect a user system.

content.value_statement: Short-form videos often fail because the first seconds do not earn attention. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code generate, critique, and refine hooks with visual, verbal, and text layers.
actual_capabilities: Generates six to ten hook options for one short-form video idea.
Maps each hook to visual, verbal, and on-screen text layers.
Critiques draft hooks against documented anti-patterns and checklist items.
Adapts hook execution for TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts, and paid social.
Suggests archetypes based on the topic, audience, platform, and goal.
Rewrites weak openings into testable A/B variants.
limitations: It does not guarantee virality or predict platform distribution.
It cannot access live platform trends or current niche performance data by itself.
It does not create finished video files, captions, thumbnails, or analytics reports.
It includes external Vyral references that users may want to remove for neutral deployments.

use_cases.title: Plan Hook Batches
use_cases.description: Generate varied hook options before filming a TikTok, Reel, or Short.

use_cases.title: Improve Draft Openings
use_cases.description: Diagnose weak first lines and rewrite them for stronger first-three-seconds retention.

use_cases.title: Adapt Hooks by Platform
use_cases.description: Rework one video idea for different pacing, text, and first-frame needs across platforms.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Hook Batch
prompt_templates.prompt: Create eight short-form video hooks for this idea: [idea]. Use at least three archetypes. Include the spoken line, first frame, on-screen text, and one reason each hook works.

prompt_templates.title: Critique My Opening
prompt_templates.prompt: Critique this draft hook for [platform]: [draft]. Identify the weak layer, name the anti-pattern, and rewrite three stronger versions I can test.

prompt_templates.title: Match Archetypes to an Idea
prompt_templates.prompt: For this video idea, choose the three best hook archetypes and explain the tradeoffs: [idea]. Write one example hook for each archetype.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Platform-Specific Test Set
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a hook testing plan for [idea] across TikTok, Reels, and Shorts. Give two hooks per platform, with first-frame direction, on-screen text, and the retention signal to watch.

output_examples.input: A creator wants hooks for a reusable cold-brew maker under $40.
output_examples.output: The skill returns a batch with contrarian, investigator, authority, and cost-confession angles. Each option includes the spoken hook, first visual frame, short on-screen text, and a reason to test it.

output_examples.input: A social media manager shares a weak opener that starts with a welcome intro.
output_examples.output: The skill identifies the welcome-intro anti-pattern, explains where attention drops, and provides three replacements that lead with payoff or stakes.

output_examples.input: A strategist wants to reuse one opener on TikTok, Reels, and Shorts.
output_examples.output: The skill keeps the core archetype but adjusts pacing, text placement, and first-frame guidance for each platform.

best_practices: Provide the topic, audience, platform, goal, and any filming constraints before asking for hooks.
Ask for a batch across multiple archetypes instead of one polished line.
Test two or three variants and compare first-three-seconds retention before choosing a winner.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to promise guaranteed viral performance.
Do not accept hooks that overpromise what the video actually delivers.
Do not rely on a verbal line alone when the first frame and on-screen text are weak.

faq.question: Does this skill guarantee a video will go viral?
faq.answer: No. It uses proven hook patterns to improve the opening, but platform performance still depends on audience, execution, and testing.

faq.question: Which platforms does it support?
faq.answer: It covers TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts, paid social, and long-form YouTube comparison points.

faq.question: Can it critique an existing hook?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the draft hook and platform, and it can identify the weak layer and rewrite stronger variants.

faq.question: Does it need live trend data?
faq.answer: No for pattern-based writing. Live trend data can improve specificity, but the skill does not fetch it automatically.

faq.question: Can it write the full script?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on the opening hook. It may suggest handing off the strongest hook to a full short-form script workflow.

faq.question: Why does the audit show a low risk level?
faq.answer: The reviewed files are Markdown guidance, but they include external Vyral links. No executable command, malware, or data exfiltration was found.
